revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:八王子神社 (恵那市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hachioji Shrine | image = File:Akechi Hachioji-jinja 2021-01 ac (1).jpg | location = [[Akechi-cho, Ena|Akechi Town]], {{ill|Ena, Gifu|en|Ena, Gifu|qid=Q819653|lt=Ena City|1=Ena, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.30611|N|137.39111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto]]
[[Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto]]
[[Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto]]
[[Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kumanokusubi-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tagorihime|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]] | shrine_Rank = [[Modern shrine Ranking system|Former Village shrine]], [[Kinpei-sha]] (*Gold-enshrined shrine*) | founded = [[949 AD]] (3rd year of [[Emperor Murakami|Emperor Murakami's]] Tenryaku era) | main_hall = | annual_festival = Early October | rituals = | corporate_number = 6200005009449 | prefecture= {{ill|Ena|qid=Q819653|1=Ena, Gifu}} }} '''Hachioji Shrine''' (八王子神社, *Hachioji Jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in '''Akechi Town, Ena City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan'''. It is a '''Kinpei-sha (金幣社)''', officially recognized by the '''Gifu Prefecture Shrine Association'''. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the '''eight divine sons of Amaterasu Ōmikami''': * '''Male deities''': [[Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto]], [[Ame-no-Hohi|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto]], [[Amatsuhikone-no-Mikoto]], [[Ikutsuhikone-no-Mikoto]], [[Kumanokusubi-no-Mikoto]] * '''Female deities''': [[Tagorihime|Tagorihime-no-Mikoto]], [[Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto]] == Festivals & Events == The '''annual grand festival''' is held in early '''October'''. During the festival, '''five floats''' (*yatai*)—'''Miyamoto-gumi, Wakai-gumi, Kikyo-ren, Kodomo-gumi, and Seinen-dan'''—parade through central Akechi Town before gathering at the shrine. == History == Hachioji Shrine was founded in '''949 AD (Tenryaku 3)'''. At its peak, it held '''2,000 koku of shrine land'''. Before the '''Akechi-Toyama clan''' took control in the early Kamakura period, the shrine governed '''Akechi, Takanami, Kushihara, and Asatani villages'''. During the '''Tensho era (1574 AD)''', the shrine was destroyed during '''Takeda Katsuyori’s invasion of Tōnō''' in the '''Siege of Akechi Castle'''. Shrine priest '''Tango Negi''' and his attendants attempted to defend the shrine and castle, fighting alongside '''Lord Toyama Ikko (Yosuke)'''. Tango, a master archer, killed several enemy soldiers before retreating to the mountains, where he succumbed to his wounds. The shrine was burned down by Takeda’s forces. Afterward, the shrine remained in a diminished state until '''1637 AD (Kan'ei 14)''', when '''Toyama Kanzemon''', the '''daimyō of Akechi Jinya''' and a '''Hatamoto (Kōtaiyoriai)''', prayed to Hachioji Shrine for an heir. When his prayers were answered, he '''donated 50 koku of land from Kosugi Village''' and '''rebuilt the shrine'''. In '''1676 AD (Enpō 4)''', '''Toyama Yoshitsugu''', the fourth-generation Hatamoto daimyō of Akechi, reconstructed the '''current shrine buildings'''. The construction required '''700 ryō''' and involved: * '''7,347 carpenters''' * '''632 sawyers''' * '''1,630 thatchers''' * '''194 painters''' == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Akechi Hachioji-jinja 2021-01 ac (3).jpg|thumb|The '''Shrine Gate (Shinmon)'''—a Gifu Prefecture-designated cultural property]] The shrine grounds contain: * '''A柿本人麻呂神社 (Kakinomoto Shrine)''', which is believed to have been founded by '''Akechi Mitsuhide'''. * '''A maple tree''', said to have been planted by '''Akechi Mitsuhide'''. === '''Shinmon (Shrine Gate)''' === The shrine gate was reportedly '''moved from Akechi Castle''' (*Shiratakajō*). It is built in the '''Mukakaramon style''' (向唐門), with a '''copper plate roof''', dating back to the '''early Edo period'''. It is designated as a '''Gifu Prefecture-designated cultural property'''. === '''Main Shrine Buildings''' === The shrine's architecture follows the '''Gongen-zukuri''' style, combining the '''Honden (main hall), Haiden (worship hall), and Heiden (offering hall)''' into one structure. The buildings include: * '''Main Hall (Honden)''': Three-bay *nagare-zukuri* style, copper-plated roof (formerly bark-thatch) * '''Offering Hall (Heiden)''': *Ryōge-zukuri* style, 3-bay width, 2-bay depth * '''Worship Hall (Haiden)''': *Irimoya-zukuri* style, copper-plated roof, 7-bay width, 4-bay depth The shrine also houses '''seven Edo-period ema (votive paintings)''', depicting various scenes such as '''"Yukai no Zu" (遊鹿図), "Takasu no Zu" (高砂図), "Shinme no Zu" (神馬図), and "Rashomon no Zu" (羅生門図)'''. These paintings were '''donated by the Toyama clan''' and designated as '''Gifu Prefecture cultural properties''' in 1974. == '''Branch Shrines & Influence''' == Hachioji Shrine was historically the '''main shrine of the Akechi Toyama clan''', overseeing '''26 branch shrines across 20 villages'''. Shrine priests from these branch shrines would gather at Hachioji Shrine for '''annual ceremonies'''. Notable branch shrines include: * '''Hachioji Shrine (Sugino, Akechi)''' * '''Hachioji Shrine (Kirami, Akechi)''' * '''Hachiman Shrine (Kirami, Akechi)''' * '''Hachiman Shrine (Sugino, Akechi)''' * '''Kumano Shrine (Noshi, Akechi)''' * '''Kasuga Shrine (Oizumi, Akechi)''' * '''Sumiyoshi Shrine (Azuma, Akechi)''' * '''Tennō Shrine (Kamite, Yamaoka)''' * '''Hachioji Shrine (Toyota, Aichi)''' * '''Hachioji Shrine (Shimokiri, Toyota, Aichi)''' * '''Hachioji Shrine (Asatani, Toyota, Aichi)''' == '''Designated Cultural Properties & Natural Monuments''' == === '''Gifu Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties''' === * '''Hachioji Shrine Buildings (1676, Enpō 4)''' – designated '''December 10, 1975''' * '''Seven Edo-period votive paintings (Ema)''' – designated '''March 6, 1974''' == External Links == {{Commonscat|Hachioji-jinja (Akechi-cho, Ena)}} * [https://www.kankou-ena.jp/4325.php Hachioji Shrine] - Ena City Official Tourism Site * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_5832.html Hachioji Shrine] - Gifu Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/3827.html Hachioji Shrine Buildings] - Gifu Prefecture * [https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12389.html Edo-period Ema] - Gifu Prefecture == References == * '''Akechi Town History''' (明智町誌), Akechi Town, 1960, pp. 370-375 * '''Akechi Jinya Records''' (明知御陣屋), Toyama Yoshitsugu's Equestrian Archery at the Festival, 2009, p. 81 {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|八王子神社 (恵那市)|version=103496395|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Hachioji-jinja (Akechi-cho, Ena)}} [[ja:八王子神社 (恵那市)]] {{wikidata link|Q119929597}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ena]] [[Category:Ena, Gifu]] [[Category:History of Ena]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōyama Clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
